response.justification: Strong current alignment with AI-native engineering: present role at re:cinq explicitly covers MCP code generators and agentic workflow tooling, which directly matches the role's baseline expectation. High-throughput exposure from Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/s microservices) and a long, polyglot track record support the distributed-systems requirement. However, Python — a must-have — is absent from the listed skills, the title progression is mostly Senior rather than Staff (only current role is Staff, ~1 year), and on-call/incident-response and stakeholder-management experience are not evidenced. Worth an interview to probe Python depth, distributed-systems decision scope, and operational ownership.
strengths: Hands-on with MCP and agentic workflow tooling in current Staff role at re:cinq
Prior work on microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/second (Sociomantic) with low-latency async I/O
Strong TypeScript/Node.js background plus TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development discipline
concerns: Python not listed in skills despite being a must-have for the role
Only ~1 year at Staff level; most tenure is Senior-titled IC work
No explicit evidence of on-call rotation, incident response, or cross-functional stakeholder leadership
